response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and web-platform engineer with a strong career trajectory (Google Staff Engineer), but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role on every critical axis. The hard requirement for native-level Japanese proficiency is not met — he lists English, Romanian, and German only. There is no evidence of C-based embedded or security-feature development, no familiarity with LAN-switch internals, and no demonstrated experience with the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, MAC authentication). His background is overwhelmingly web, cloud, and data-platform oriented, which does not transfer to embedded firmware security engineering.
strengths: Extensive and proven software engineering career spanning 15+ years with progressive seniority, currently at Staff level at Google
Some foundational embedded and low-level experience: C/C++ work at National Instruments, low-latency async I/O at Dunnhumby, and Linux-based audio systems in academic work
Strong DevOps and tooling discipline (CI/CD, Docker, GitLab, trunk-based development) indicating solid engineering practices
concerns: Does not meet the hard Japanese language requirement — zero Japanese proficiency listed, making this an immediate disqualifier
No security domain experience whatsoever: no MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, authentication systems, or any network-security feature development
No LAN-switch, embedded networking, Linux kernel modification, or chip-vendor SDK experience; the resume is almost entirely web and cloud stack

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security feature experience
